definitions: To be pursuing a mistaken course of action.
To be making a wrong choice.
To be accusing the wrong person.
examples: If you think I stole your lunch, you're barking up the wrong tree.
She's barking up the wrong tree if she expects me to help her cheat.
He's barking up the wrong tree trying to fix that old car; it's beyond repair.
source: you're barking up the wrong tree
